titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the First Baptist Church of Marion records, 1804-1904.
First Baptist Church of Marion records, 1804-1904

titlepage.titleproper: Guide to the First Baptist Church Of Marion Records,1804-1904.

did.head: DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY
did.unittitle: First Baptist Church of Marion records,

origination.label: Creator:
origination.corpname: First Baptist Church of Marion (Marion, N.Y.)

did.repository: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library
did.abstract: Two volumes of quarterly church covenant and special meeting minutes (1804-1846, 1848-1893); one volume of trustee's minutes (1824-1893) which also contains treasurer's reports (1896-1904) and membership records; and meeting minutes and membership and dues lists for the church's Female Benevolent Society. Church discipline, revivals, anti-masonry, anti-slaveryand missionary activity figure prominently as subjects.

bioghist.head: ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
bioghist.p: The First Baptist Church of Marion was organized as the First Baptist Church of Williamson on February 24, 1804 by Elder Seba Norton of Sodus, New York with nineteen original members. During the next twenty-five years services were held regularly at the Mason schoolhouse. In 1829 the Society built the first church building; in Williamson, a wooden structure with galleries on three sides. In 1850 the church was converted into a store and a new building, costing $4000, was erected and dedicated on November 25, 1850. In 1867 an addition and extensive repairs were made. See The History of Wayne County and the accessions folder for a list of pastors and additional details.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: This collection contains three volumes of church records: two volumes of quarterly church covenant and special meeting minutes (1804-1846, 1848-1893); one volume of trustees' minutes (1824-1893), also containing treasurer's reports (1896-1904) and membership records; and "The Secretary's Book of the Female Benevolent Society" of the church (consisting of meeting minutes and membership and dues lists). Church discipline and doctrine, revivals and Anti-Masonry figure prominently as subjects of the covenant meetings; the slavery question and missionary activity were also discussed.

controlaccess.head: Names:
controlaccess.corpname: First Baptist Church of Marion (Marion, N.Y.)
First Baptist Church of Williamson (Williamson, N.Y.)

scopecontent.p: Contains minutes of monthly covenant quarterly church and sporadic special meetings which dealt with church membership, discipline, pastorship, and finance. Located prior to the minutes in this volume is: a catalogue of members (February 1838); a treasurer's account of money collected for the Baptist Missionary Society of Marion listing names and amounts (n.d.); the church's. Platform and Confession of Faith; and a record of deaths (I8l4-l8, 1835-38). Minutes commence on January 9, 1804. Items of note, with the exception of disciplinary cases and revivals which are too numerous to note, follow:

Doctrinal questions are discussed, male and female members are listed 97 P. with note as to whether they died or were dismissed, and a list of members dismissed from the Baptist Church in Palmyra to form this church.
Delegates sent to the formation of a church in Sodus.
Policy on adultery and remarriage of the innocent party.
Member expelled for joining a pedobaptist church.
(out of order) an ordination is planned.
Bro. expelled for pedobaptist beliefs.
Revival.
Doctrinal disagreement over the Trinity
Bro. expelled for anti-trinitarian beliefs.
Sr. recalls her baptisms into the Arian Church (Christian Society .
The church begins plans to build a church.
The church votes to call itself "Baptist Church at Marion"
Bro. expelled for bigamy.
Long controversy over the theft of a shawl.
Nov and almost every winter until the Civil War occur large numbers of baptisms.
Anti-Hasonry resolution and another resolution to build a meeting house.
Bro. dismissed for being a Mason.
Voted to petition the Commissioner of Highways to lay a road to the meetinghouse.
First communion in the new meetinghouse.
Voted to adopt the resolution passed by the Whitesborough Conference in relation to Freemasonry.
Voted to leave the Ontario Baptist Association to join the Wayne Baptist Association.
A letter is sent to Elder Weaver approving his course in managing protracted meetings.
Former member of the Walworth Free Will Baptists joins the church.
A resolution on home and foreign missions is passed in response to a letter from the American and Foreign Bible Society.
Council called to try a former minister. Discussion continues from January - August.
Membership of 188 is reported.
Brother is expelled for his "infidel" principles.
Anti-Slavery resolution. The church has 332 members.
Minutes end.

Contains minutes of monthly covenant, quarterly church and occasional special meetings which deal with church membership, discipline, pastorship and finance. At the front of the volume before the meeting minutes is a membership list (5 p.) indicating how members came into and left the church. Revivals, which occurred almost every winter during February - April are not indicated. Items of note follow: (Note: April 1846 - May 1848 is missing.)
Anti-Odd-Fellow resolution.
Bro. excluded for denying the immortality of the soul.
Voted to buy the Marion Collegiate Institute for $5,000.
Trustees elected normall reported in Item 1.
Elder George Balcona labors with the church.
A Dutch Reformed pastor, M.V., is baptised with members of his congregation, and licensed to preach.
A council is called to consider organizing M.V.
The church adopts the "American Baptist Publication Society Covenant . "
Eleven members including M.V. , are set off as "The Holland Branch of the Baptist Church in Marion."
For the first time women are appointed to serve in the church.
M.V. suspended for violation of the 7th commandment. A sister also expelled for the same reason.
M.V. defrocked and expelled.
Plans to take a collection for Rochester University.
Rules of order adopted.
Several lists of members indicating date of baptism or letter, date restored, dismissed, excluded, dropped or died, one for 1880.
An agreement between the church and Alonzo G. Potter to have Potter donate an organ to the church in memory of his parents, former deacons of the church.
A list of members and amounts of money.
Minutes of a meeting held on January l6, 1888.
More names and amounts.

did.unittitle: Trustee's Records, (1824-1893) and Annual Meeting Minutebook

scopecontent.p: Part 2 is a copy of part 1 from 1828-1850 with additional records. Meetings were more frequent during the construction of meeting houses.

did.unittitle: Part 3: End of vol. "Treasurer's report of the church,
The Secretary's Book of the Female Benevolent Society of Marion Baptiste Church Wayene Co. N.Y. 1857, (1857-1876) .

scopecontent.p: Contains meeting minutes beginning with the 7th Annual meeting. They include date and location of meeting, names of members and dues paid, the Constitution of the Society and items purchased.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on use:
userestrict.p: Not to be published or otherwise released in such form that any individual mentioned in the records can be identified.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: First Baptist Church of Marion records, #6063. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
